title: teucrium orientale var. puberulens
intro: Teucrium orientale var. puberulens, also known as the hairy wood mint or hairy germander, is a variant of the Teucrium orientale plant, which belongs to the Lamiaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Teucrium orientale var. puberulens include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine. The plant can be prepared in various ways, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
